entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: The legume crop being evaluated for resistance to waterlogging stress.

entities.name: WATERLOGGING RESISTANCE
entities.description: The capacity of a plant to withstand and survive saturated soil conditions.

entities.name: POT TECHNIQUE
entities.description: A screening method involving the growth of plants in pots with specific media to evaluate stress response.

entities.name: WATERLOGGING
entities.description: An environmental condition where soil is saturated with water, depriving roots of oxygen.

entities.name: ROOT DRY MASS
entities.description: The mass of the root system after drying, used to measure the impact of waterlogging.

entities.name: SHOOT DRY MASS
entities.description: The mass of the above-ground plant parts after drying, used to measure the impact of waterlogging.

entities.name: PLANT SURVIVAL
entities.description: The ability of the plant to remain alive following a period of waterlogging.

entities.name: MORTALITY
entities.description: The percentage of plants that die under waterlogging stress.

entities.name: GROWTH MEDIUM
entities.description: The soil or substrate used in pots to grow the plants during the screening process.

entities.name: PRELIMINARY SCREENING
entities.description: The initial process of evaluating a large number of cultivars to identify those with desired traits.
